_comment: BCS Rubric Configuration — v1.0.0. This is the single source of truth for all dimension definitions and weights. /bcs/score reads from here. When the rubric updates, only this file changes — no API contract changes, no breaking updates.

dimensions.label: Personality Hook
dimensions.description: Does the adopter know who this dog IS — not just breed and age, but personality? Is there one specific moment or detail that makes this dog stick in someone's mind?

scores.2: Specific, memorable, only-this-dog detail. Makes you feel something.
scores.1: Some warmth, but generic. Could describe any dog of this breed.

dimensions.label: Visual Impact — Photos
dimensions.description: Do the photos make someone stop scrolling? Dog's face visible? Real light, real expression, real life?

scores.2: 3+ photos: face-on with eye contact, full body, in motion or with a human.
scores.1: 1–2 photos, decent quality, but limited angles or expression.
scores.0: Single shot, blurry, kennel background, dog facing away.

dimensions.label: Video Presence
dimensions.description: Does a video exist? Does it make you feel something in the first 10 seconds?

scores.2: Video exists, dog's personality is visible, authentic not staged, emotionally engaging.
scores.1: Video exists but opens on a wall, or is low-energy, or too long with no hook.

dimensions.label: Compatibility Clarity
dimensions.description: Can the RIGHT adopter self-identify immediately? And the wrong adopter self-select out? Kids, other dogs, cats, energy level, home type — stated clearly and honestly.

scores.2: Clear, honest, specific. Right adopter knows immediately. Wrong adopter knows too.
scores.1: Some compatibility noted, but incomplete or vague.
scores.0: Nothing stated — adopter has to guess.

dimensions.label: Foster Voice
dimensions.description: Does this listing feel like a real person who knows and loves this dog — or does it feel like a form was filled out?

scores.2: Personal, warm, specific details only the foster would know. You can feel the relationship.
scores.1: Some personality in the writing, but still leans generic or template-like.
scores.0: Clinical, factual, vet-record language. No human warmth.

dimensions.label: No Surprises
dimensions.description: Are any challenges or special needs communicated clearly — without being scary or unnecessarily disqualifying?

scores.2: Honest, specific, reframed positively. Right home recognizes themselves.
scores.1: Challenges mentioned but framed in a way that sounds worse than it is.
scores.0: Challenges omitted entirely, OR framed so negatively the dog sounds unadoptable.

dimensions.label: Story-First Gate
dimensions.description: Is a coached intro video ready? Can someone feel this dog before they ever request a live meet?

scores.2: Short, coached, warm intro video ready — dog's personality visible in under 60 seconds.
scores.1: Some intro video exists but uncoached, low energy, or too long.
scores.0: No intro video — adopters arrive cold.

dimensions.label: Presenter Readiness
dimensions.description: Does the presenter know this dog? Have they read the coaching packet? Do they know the strongest asset and the likely questions?

scores.2: Presenter can speak to specific moments, addresses compatibility naturally, knows what to lead with.
scores.1: Presenter knows the basics but is likely to read from the bio or get caught off guard.
scores.0: Presenter meeting this dog for the first time on camera.

dimensions.label: Family Vision
dimensions.description: After seeing this listing — photos, video, description — can the adopter picture this dog in their life? Not 'this seems like a nice dog' but 'that's my dog.'

scores.2: Listing creates a picture. You can see the dog at the end of your bed, in your car, on your couch.
scores.1: Listing is warm but stays abstract. Describes the dog without making you feel the dog.
scores.0: Listing is invisible. No image forms. No feeling.

A+.label: Broadcast ready. Present this dog now.

A.label: Strong. Minor gaps — coach on 1–2 dimensions before live.

B.label: Decent foundation. Real coaching needed on 3–4 dimensions.

C.label: Significant gaps. Don't present yet — story needs work.

D.label: Start over. This dog is invisible right now.
